candidates.evidence_quote: profitability surpassing expectations, resulting in 24% year-over-year growth in adjusted earnings per share, the third consecutive quarter of 20% or greater growth
Total revenues of $904 million finished towards the higher end of the range we provided
For Q3, we anticipate total revenues to be between $855 and $870 million, which represents year-over-year growth similar to Q2
adjusted net income margin of 5.9% well exceeded the high end of the guidance we provided
As such, we are increasing our net income margin projections for the full year 2024
Comparable sales increased 1.4% versus the prior year
North Italia's second quarter comparable sales increased 2% from the prior year
we ended a negative 0.2% on traffic
cost of sales decreased 90 basis points, primarily driven by higher menu pricing than commodity inflation
The pricing was 4.5 and the mix was a negative 2.9
I think we've seen a little bit of alcohol trade down, which has resulted in that
we expect effective commodity inflation of low single digits for Q3 as our broad market basket remains very stable
We are modeling net total labor inflation of mid-single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases
factoring in the latest trends in wage rates and minimum wage increases, as well as other components of labor
We're also not really seeing any impact yet on wages at all from the FAST Act
primarily driven by higher menu pricing than labor inflation and labor productivity improvements
they drove year-over-year improvements in food efficiencies, labor productivity, overtime, and wage management
we continued to produce year-over-year improvements in both manager and hourly staff retention
the Cheesecake Factory restaurant's four-wall margin was 17.7% for the quarter. the highest level in the past six years
Annualized unit volumes at the Cheesecake Factory reached 12.5 million for the quarter
Cheesecake Factory off-premise sales remained relatively stable, 21% of sales for the second quarter
And external bakery sales were $13.6 million, down 12% from the prior year
So just a little bit of pressure on the, you know, branded retail. I mean, I think you've seen that from other big CPG companies
Flower Child sales totaled $35.7 million, up 7% from the prior year, and sales per operating week were $85,900
Other FRC sales totaled $73.6 million, up 12% from the prior year, and sales per operating week were $134,000
we still plan to open as many as 22 new restaurants this year across our portfolio of concepts, with four to five openings in the third quarter
Pre-opening costs were $7 million in the quarter, compared to $6 million in the prior year period
we would anticipate approximately $180 million to $200 million in CapEx to support this year's and some of next year's unit development
During the quarter, we completed approximately $3.9 million in share repurchases
returned $13.9 million to shareholders via our dividend
we incorporated two Cheesecake Factory restaurant closures, one of which closed mid-July related to a lease exit
G&A decreased 40 basis points, mostly driven by lower legal fees
we obviously are spending more on marketing because of the rewards program
we remain encouraged by the level of member activity and engagement that we are seeing
virtually every key on-premise and off-premise net promoter score metric that we track for the Cheesecake Factory ending the quarter at an historic high
we updated sales projections for some of our lesser-established concepts, which are performing more in line with broader casual dining sales trends
it was tremendously hot in parts of the Northeast, all across the country, really. I think that did impact the patio usage
as hot as it has been in so many parts of the country, it can impact patio utilization
we recorded a pre-tax net expense of $1 million, primarily related to FRC acquisition-related expenses and impairment of assets and lease terminations income
primarily related to FRC acquisition-related expenses

notes: Chunk contains a single event: Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript, part 1 of 2 (prepared remarks plus Q&A through UBS); names coined only from this visible part.
fiscal_kpis is null in this chunk, so no KPI-derived candidates.

Cheesecake Factory brand metrics (comps, traffic, four-wall margin, AUV, off-premise) are named at company level (same_store_sales, restaurant_traffic, restaurant_level_margin, average_unit_volume, off_premise_sales) because the brand name equals the company legal name, which is banned inside driver_name.

fox_restaurant_concepts_sales uses the source's reporting-segment label (Other FRC sales); flagged because Fox Restaurant Concepts is also an acquired subsidiary name - reconciler may prefer a segment-neutral form.

Skipped as vague/routine/derivative: tough-and-transitory consumer environment, hybrid-work/seasonality speculation, California consumer trends (reported as no change), debt/liquidity levels, depreciation dollar guidance, G&A dollar guidance, enterprise-vs-brand margin gap (derivative of restaurant_level_margin plus new_unit_development), new-opening demand anecdotes (folded into new_unit_development).
